text.title: wbs-creation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for wbs-creation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: WBS without a Dictionary — just a numbered outline.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Numbers without definitions leave acceptance criteria, ownership, and scope boundaries undefined. The tree looks complete but is not actionable.

failure-mode.description: Mixing WBS with schedule — adding start/end dates to nodes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The WBS answers "what"; the schedule answers "when". Mixing them creates churn when either changes independently.

failure-mode.description: Decomposing unevenly — 4 levels for development, 1 level for deployment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Uneven depth signals that some branches are under-estimated; both depth and estimate accuracy should be consistent across siblings.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to activity verbs; explicitly instruct "use noun phrases for deliverables".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without explicit instruction, agents generate activity-based WBS that breaks on implementation changes.

failure-mode.description: Agents skip overhead packages (PM, QA, deploy, train) — hard-code them in the prompt as required.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents default to product deliverables; overhead must be explicitly mandated.

failure-mode.description: Decomposition often has uneven depth: backend gets 4 levels, "Marketing" stays at level 2. Force the agent to balance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Uneven depth hides estimate errors in under-decomposed branches.

failure-mode.description: Dictionary entries get hallucinated owners and dates; force them to come from a stakeholder register and schedule input.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Made-up owners and dates destroy traceability and accountability.

failure-mode.description: Agents inflate estimates symmetrically (everything is 40h); inject a few known-good estimates as anchors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Anchoring prevents uniform inflation and calibrates agent estimates to real data.

failure-mode.description: Do not let agents "consolidate" leaves to fit a target count — that breaks 8/80; reject and re-prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Consolidation violates 8/80 rule; agents use it to artificially meet targets.

failure-mode.description: When converting WBS to tasks, beware ID drift: keep wbs_id field in every generated task for round-tripping.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lost traceability between WBS and tasks makes re-planning impossible.
